backlog.title: markdownBareAtImports — Optional Bare-@ Markdown Imports (PRD §4.6)
backlog.description: Implements PRD §4.6 — the single opt-in config setting (markdownBareAtImports) that makes a delivered markdown file treat a bare @<path> (no leading #) as an import, with every rule that applies to #@ imports (relative-only resolution, extension shorthand, code-exempt, dedup, paging, budget). REALITY CHECK: the rest of the PRD (core #@ injection, text/image/binary classification, paged delivery, markdown transitive imports, extension shorthand, code-region detection, total-size budget accounting, and the #@ path autocomplete provider) is ALREADY implemented and passing in file-injector.ts (879 lines, 92 tests green, tsc --strict clean) — see plan/005_b7c78dbf73b1/architecture/system_context.md. This phase adds the config system + bare-@ matching, verifies the whole PRD, and syncs docs. The existing robustness improvements over the PRD §9 pseudocode (Unicode regex, magic-number image sniff, empty-image note, Step 3.5 existence pre-check, surrogate-safe headSlice) MUST be preserved, not reverted. Every implementation subtask follows implicit TDD: write the failing test into file-injector.test.mjs → implement → pass.

milestones.title: Bare-@ Engine + Config Foundation (building blocks)
milestones.description: Two independent, self-contained building blocks (each TDD-complete with its own unit tests): (1) extend the scanTokens choke point to ALSO match bare @<path> when an opt-in flag is set, carrying a prefixLen so downstream stripping knows whether to remove 1 or 2 marker chars; (2) add the config foundation (verified exports CONFIG_DIR_NAME + getAgentDir, the FileInjectorConfig type, and a pure readConfig loader). Neither block touches the pipeline wiring yet — that is M2.

tasks.title: Extend scanTokens for bare-@ matching (BARE_AT_RE + prefixLen) + unit tests
tasks.description: scanTokens is the single choke point for ALL #@/bare-@ detection (top-level prompt AND markdown content). Add the BARE_AT_RE constant, a prefixLen field on returned records, a bareAt opt, and restructure the match loop to union FILE_INJECT_RE and BARE_AT_RE candidates when bareAt is on. Pure addition — behavior is byte-for-byte identical when bareAt===false.

subtasks.title: Add BARE_AT_RE + prefixLen + restructure scanTokens to union #@ and bare-@ matches (with unit tests)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/005_b7c78dbf73b1/architecture/codebase_delta.md §1 and §5. The existing scanTokens (exported, async — it stats candidate paths during resolution) is at file-injector.ts. It currently has signature scanTokens(text, baseDir, opts: {allowAbsTilde, skipCode, tryMdExt}, state) returning Promise<{index, abs}[]>. The shipped FILE_INJECT_RE is Unicode-aware: /(^|(?<![\p{L}\p{N}_]))#@(\S+)/gu — an intentional improvement over the PRD §4.2 (?<=\W) form; PRESERVE it.
2. INPUT: The existing scanTokens function, FILE_INJECT_RE, cleanToken, isAbsoluteOrTilde, resolveImportPath, computeCodeRanges, inCode (all already implemented and unchanged). No new state fields needed here (bareAt comes via opts, not State).
3. LOGIC: (a) Add BARE_AT_RE. PRD §4.6 literal: /(^|(?<=[^\w#]))@(\S+)/g. RECOMMENDATION for consistency with the shipped Unicode FILE_INJECT_RE: use /(^|(?<![\p{L}\p{N}_#]))@(\S+)/gu (Unicode \p{} classes + explicit # in the negated lookbehind + u flag) so user@host.com and #@file are both excluded in any language (café@x, 日本語@x). Either form is acceptable — document the choice in a comment. CRITICAL (§4.6): the lookbehind MUST forbid a preceding '#', so #@file is matched once by FILE_INJECT_RE and NEVER by BARE_AT_RE (no double-match). (b) Add bareAt:boolean to the opts param. (c) Add prefixLen:number to the returned record type → Promise<{index, prefixLen, abs}[]>. (d) Restructure the loop: build a cands array — push {idx:m.index!, token:m[2], prefixLen:2} for every FILE_INJECT_RE match; when opts.bareAt, ALSO push {idx:m.index!, token:m[2], prefixLen:1} for every BARE_AT_RE match; sort cands by idx; then run the EXISTING per-candidate body (code-region skip via inCode, cleanToken, allowAbsTilde check, resolveImportPath, injectedSet/localSeen dedup) unchanged, emitting {index:c.idx, prefixLen:c.prefixLen, abs}. Dedup keys on the RESOLVED abs (so #@api.md and @api.md in one file collapse to one injection). Mock nothing (scanTokens only stats real temp files in tests).
4. OUTPUT: scanTokens now accepts opts.bareAt and returns records with prefixLen. Behavior is BYTE-FOR-BYTE IDENTICAL when bareAt===false (the existing 92 tests must still pass without changes to their scanTokens calls). Consumed by P1.M2.T1.S1 (processTokenStream + injectFiles) and P1.M2.T2.S1 (injectMarkdown).
5. TEST (implicit TDD — write failing test → implement → pass, into file-injector.test.mjs): unit-test scanTokens directly with a fresh State (empty injectedSet, remaining:null). Cases: (a) bareAt:false on markdown content containing @api.md (file exists) → returns [] (no bare-@ match). (b) bareAt:true → returns one record {prefixLen:1} for @api.md; a sibling #@other.md record has prefixLen:2. (c) no-double-match: '#@file' with bareAt:true → exactly ONE candidate (prefixLen:2), BARE_AT_RE does NOT also match it. (d) user@host.com in content with bareAt:true → not matched (@ mid-word). (e) dedup: @api.md and #@api.md in same scan → one resolved record (whichever sorts first), the second dropped. (f) code-exempt: @api.md inside a fenced block with skipCode:true → not matched.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] Add/update the JSDoc on scanTokens to document the new bareAt opt and the prefixLen field (prefixLen=2 for #@, 1 for a bare @). Rides WITH the work — do not create a separate docs subtask.

tasks.title: Config foundation (imports + FileInjectorConfig + readConfig) + unit tests + guard sync
tasks.description: Add the pure config-reading foundation that §4.6 depends on: import the VERIFIED exports CONFIG_DIR_NAME and getAgentDir, declare the FileInjectorConfig type, and implement readConfig (global → project-if-trusted shallow merge, malformed/missing → default {}). Standalone, exported, never-throws — no factory/session_start wiring yet (that is M2). Also add readConfig to the test harness module-surface guard.

subtasks.title: Add CONFIG_DIR_NAME/getAgentDir imports + FileInjectorConfig + readConfig function (with unit tests + ASSERTED_EXPORTS guard update)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/005_b7c78dbf73b1/architecture/api_verification.md §1-2 and codebase_delta.md §2-3. VERIFIED: CONFIG_DIR_NAME === '.pi' (dist/config.js:394) and getAgentDir():string → ~/.pi/agent (dist/config.d.ts:75), both re-exported at dist/index.d.ts:2. The existing import line is `import { resizeImage, formatDimensionNote, type ResizedImage } from "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent";`. The test harness module-surface guard (~L132-151) lists every shipped function in ASSERTED_EXPORTS and fails if a shipped function is in NEITHER ASSERTED_EXPORTS nor PURE_HELPERS_NOT_ASSERTED — a new export MUST be added or the gate fails.
2. INPUT: The existing imports section of file-injector.ts and the existing ASSERTED_EXPORTS guard in file-injector.test.mjs. No runtime dependencies beyond the already-used node:fs, node:path.
3. LOGIC: (a) Add CONFIG_DIR_NAME and getAgentDir to the existing @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent import. (b) Add `interface FileInjectorConfig { markdownBareAtImports?: boolean; }`. (c) Add an EXPORTED `readConfig(ctx: { cwd: string; isProjectTrusted: () => boolean }): Promise<FileInjectorConfig>` per PRD §9 readConfig pseudocode: a tryRead helper that JSON.parses the file (trim + '||"{}"' fallback for empty) and returns {} on any error; read the GLOBAL config at path.join(getAgentDir(), 'file-injector.json') first; then if ctx.isProjectTrusted() read the PROJECT config at path.join(ctx.cwd, CONFIG_DIR_NAME, 'file-injector.json') and shallow-merge it over the global (project wins). Missing or malformed either file → default {} (markdownBareAtImports undefined → treated as false downstream). NEVER throws. Prefer the narrow typed ctx param shown here (only cwd + isProjectTrusted are used) so it is unit-testable with a tiny mock — do NOT type it as `any`.
4. OUTPUT: readConfig is exported and pure (only reads JSON files); FileInjectorConfig is a type (no runtime export). Consumed by P1.M2.T1.S1 (which adds the module-level cfg var + session_start load + handler). The test harness guard now recognizes readConfig.
5. TEST (implicit TDD, into file-injector.test.mjs): unit-test readConfig directly — it takes a narrow {cwd, isProjectTrusted} so no makeMockCtx change needed (pass a literal object). Cases: (a) no config files → {}. (b) project {markdownBareAtImports:true} with isProjectTrusted()=>true → {markdownBareAtImports:true}. (c) same project config with isProjectTrusted()=>false → IGNORED (trust gate) → {}. (d) project malformed JSON (e.g. '{bad') with trusted → {} (no throw). (e) ALSO add 'readConfig' to ASSERTED_EXPORTS AND a `assert(typeof mod.readConfig === "function", ...)` line alongside the existing typeof asserts (~L113-130), so the module-surface guard passes. (Global-path merge precedence is hard to sandbox without touching ~; cover it in M2's integration test via session_start, not here.)
6. DOCS: [Mode A] Add JSDoc on readConfig documenting the two config locations, the trust gate, the shallow-merge precedence, and the malformed→default behavior (mirrors PRD §4.6). Rides WITH the work.

milestones.title: Integration Wiring (thread bareAt from config through the pipeline)
milestones.description: Wire the M1 building blocks together: add bareAt to the shared State, load config on session_start into a module-level cfg, have the input handler derive bareAt and pass it into injectFiles, thread the bareAt opt through processTokenStream (top-level = false), and update injectMarkdown to pass bareAt:state.bareAt + carry prefixLen through its Step 3.5 existence pre-check and Step 4 marker stripping. Each subtask is TDD-complete with its integration tests. After this milestone the feature is functionally complete.

tasks.title: Thread bareAt through State, the factory, and the top-level pipeline + config-loading integration test
tasks.description: The integration hub: State gains a bareAt field; the factory gains a cfg var + a session_start config load (alongside the existing autocomplete registration); the input handler derives bareAt and injectFiles sets state.bareAt; processTokenStream gains the bareAt opt and the top-level scan call passes bareAt:false. Includes an integration test that drives session_start with a temp config and verifies bareAt reaches the pipeline.

subtasks.title: State.bareAt + cfg var + session_start config load + injectFiles state init + processTokenStream bareAt opt + top-level scan call (with config-loading integration test)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See codebase_delta.md §4, §6, §7, §9. The existing factory registers pi.on("input") + pi.on("session_start") (autocomplete only). State is created INSIDE the exported injectFiles(text, imagesIn, ctx) function (not in the handler), and tests call injectFiles directly — so bareAt must reach State. injectFiles is currently stateless (no module-level reads). captureHandler(event) in the test harness can capture the session_start handler to drive config loading.
2. INPUT: readConfig (P1.M1.T2.S1) and the scanTokens bareAt opt + prefixLen (P1.M1.T1.S1). The existing injectFiles, processTokenStream, State interface, and factory.
3. LOGIC: (a) Add `bareAt: boolean` to the State interface. (b) Add module-level `let cfg: FileInjectorConfig = {};` and load it on session_start: either merge `cfg = await readConfig(ctx);` into the EXISTING session_start handler body (cleaner — one handler does autocomplete + config), or add a second session_start handler (Pi appends handlers per event; both acceptable). (c) Thread bareAt into State. DESIGN CHOICE (pick one, document it): Option A — add a `bareAt = false` param to injectFiles and have the handler pass `cfg.markdownBareAtImports === true` (cleanest for direct unit testing); Option B — have injectFiles read the module-level cfg (matches PRD §9 pseudocode where State is built in the handler, but couples injectFiles to module state). Either is acceptable; Option A is recommended for testability. (d) Add bareAt to processTokenStream's opts (passthrough to scanTokens). (e) Update the top-level processTokenStream call in injectFiles to pass bareAt:false (§4.6: bare-@ is markdown-only — the top-level prompt is unaffected). (f) IMPORTANT — the top-level marker strip in injectFiles is UNCHANGED: processTokenStream still returns number[] (just indices) and the existing `slice(0, i) + slice(i + 2)` is correct because top-level is always #@ (prefixLen 2). Do NOT add prefixLen to the top-level path.
4. OUTPUT: State carries bareAt; config is loaded on session_start; the top-level scan passes bareAt:false so existing top-level behavior is byte-for-byte identical. Consumed by P1.M2.T2.S1 (injectMarkdown) and the docs task.
5. TEST (implicit TDD, into file-injector.test.mjs): integration test of the config→pipeline path. Cases: (a) drive session_start with a mock ctx whose cwd is a temp dir containing .pi/file-injector.json {markdownBareAtImports:true} and isProjectTrusted()=>true, then invoke the input handler on a prompt whose markdown imports a bare @api.md — verify api.md IS injected (bareAt reached the pipeline). (b) Same but isProjectTrusted()=>false → api.md NOT injected (trust gate honored end-to-end). (c) Top-level safety: with bareAt effectively on, a top-level prompt '@other.md' (bare @, no #) → NOT injected (top-level is #@-only), injected=0. If the session_start capture is awkward, add an isProjectTrusted field to makeMockCtx and exercise via captureHandler('session_start'). Must not break the existing 92 cases.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the JSDoc on injectFiles / the factory's session_start to note the config load + the bareAt derivation. Rides WITH the work.

tasks.title: Wire bare-@ into the markdown import branch (injectMarkdown) + integration tests #25-28
tasks.description: injectMarkdown is the ONLY call site that passes bareAt:true (from state.bareAt). Update its scan call, carry prefixLen through the existing Step 3.5 existence pre-check, and make Step 4 marker stripping use prefixLen instead of the hardcoded 2. Includes the §11 manual-matrix integration tests (#25-28) as automated cases.

subtasks.title: injectMarkdown: pass bareAt:state.bareAt + carry prefixLen through Step 3.5 + strip with prefixLen in Step 4 (with integration tests §11 #25-28 + §10 bare-@ edges)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See codebase_delta.md §8. The existing injectMarkdown (PRIVATE — not exported; exercised via injectFiles) implements the six-step §5.6 algorithm PLUS a Step 3.5 existence pre-check (an improvement over PRD §9 pseudocode: a markdown import resolving to a missing file/dir is left verbatim rather than stripped). PRESERVE Step 3.5. The existing scan call is `scanTokens(content, dir, { allowAbsTilde: false, skipCode: true, tryMdExt: true }, state)` and Step 4 strips with hardcoded `+ 2`.
2. INPUT: scanTokens now returns {index, prefixLen, abs}[] (P1.M1.T1.S1) and accepts opts.bareAt; State now has bareAt (P1.M2.T1.S1).
3. LOGIC: Three edits in injectMarkdown. (a) Step 3 scan call: add `bareAt: state.bareAt` to the opts object. (b) Step 3.5 existence pre-check: the existing filter builds an `injectable` array by stat-ing each record; since records now carry prefixLen, the filter forwards the WHOLE record (injectable becomes {index, prefixLen, abs}[] — do NOT drop prefixLen here). (c) Step 4 strip: replace the hardcoded `stripped.slice(0, r.index) + stripped.slice(r.index + 2)` with `+ r.prefixLen` so a bare-@ import (prefixLen 1) strips just the '@' and a #@ import (prefixLen 2) strips '#@'. Step 5 (emitText on stripped content) and Step 6 (recurse) are UNCHANGED — they already operate on the resolved abs and don't care about prefixLen.
4. OUTPUT: injectMarkdown honors state.bareAt and strips the correct number of marker chars. With bareAt===false (default) behavior is byte-for-byte identical to today (the scan call gains bareAt:false which adds no BARE_AT_RE matches; prefixLen is always 2 so the strip is unchanged).
5. TEST (implicit TDD, into file-injector.test.mjs — these are the §11 #25-28 + §10 bare-@ edge cases, exercised through the full pipeline via injectFiles with bareAt on): (a) #25 default-off: notes.md with @api.md (exists) + #@notes.md, bareAt=false → @api.md NOT imported (verbatim), only notes.md, injected=1. (b) #26 on: same with bareAt=true → @api.md imported, marker stripped to api.md (prefixLen 1), injected=2. (c) #27 on + #@ still works: notes.md with #@api.md + bareAt=true → matched ONCE, injected=2. (d) #28 on + top-level unaffected: prompt '@other.md' (exists) + bareAt=true → top-level bare-@ NOT injected, injected=0. (e) §10 user@host.com in markdown (on) → not matched, verbatim. (f) §10 @username in prose (on, no username.md) → not resolved, verbatim. (g) §10 dedup: #@api.md + @api.md in same file (on) → injected ONCE, second marker verbatim. Assert a resolved bare-@ marker in the injected markdown block is stripped to just the path (no leading @ or #). Must not break the existing 92 cases.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the injectMarkdown JSDoc to note the bareAt threading and the prefixLen-aware strip. Rides WITH the work.

milestones.title: Verification Gate & Changeset-Level Documentation
milestones.description: Final whole-changeset verification gate (run the complete suite + confirm clean typecheck — the Definition-of-Done confirmation, NOT test-writing, which rode with each implementation subtask) and the cross-cutting documentation sweep (Mode B: README feature overview). Depends on every implementing subtask so it runs last.

tasks.title: Full regression + typecheck gate
tasks.description: Run the entire suite (existing 92 + all new bare-@/config cases added in M1/M2) and confirm tsc --strict is clean. This is the Definition-of-Done gate for the implementation — tests were written per-subtask (implicit TDD); this confirms they all pass together and nothing regressed.

subtasks.title: Run full suite + typecheck; confirm no regressions and injectMarkdown still private
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See system_context.md. The gate commands are `node ./file-injector.test.mjs` (exits 0/1) and `node ./scripts/typecheck.mjs` (prints 'type-checks clean under --strict (0 errors)'). The module-surface guard (ASSERTED_EXPORTS) was already updated in P1.M1.T2.S1 to include readConfig; this subtask CONFIRMS it still passes and that injectMarkdown remains NOT exported (the guard's private-function assertion).
2. INPUT: The fully-implemented, per-subtask-tested feature (P1.M1.T1.S1 + P1.M1.T2.S1 + P1.M2.T1.S1 + P1.M2.T2.S1).
3. LOGIC: (a) Run `node ./file-injector.test.mjs` → expect 0 failed (existing 92 + all new cases from M1/M2). (b) Run `node ./scripts/typecheck.mjs` → expect 'type-checks clean under --strict (0 errors)'. (c) If the typecheck surfaces a mismatch (e.g. a call site not updated for the scanTokens/processTokenStream bareAt opt), fix the offending call site — do NOT weaken strictness. (d) Confirm the module-surface guard passes (readConfig recognized; injectMarkdown still private/unexported). Mock nothing (this is a gate task).
4. OUTPUT: A green full-suite run + clean typecheck. This is the Definition-of-Done confirmation for the implementation.
5. DOCS: none — gate confirmation has no user-facing surface.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (README + overview)
tasks.description: Final Mode B doc sweep. Per-symbol JSDoc updates rode with their implementing subtasks (Mode A). This task updates the cross-cutting README overview to surface the new opt-in config capability so the delta ships with a coherent, non-stale feature description. Depends on every implementing subtask + the gate so it runs last.

subtasks.title: Add optional bare-@ markdown-imports subsection to README.md (Syntax + Limits)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: README.md already documents the full #@ feature (Syntax, Limits, What gets injected, #@ versus @). It does NOT yet mention markdownBareAtImports. The existing README prose is the tone/voice to match (concise, example-driven, a Markdown table for file types). PRD §4.6 is the spec; PRD §13.4 explains WHY it is opt-in (default off so #@ stays the only prompt-level trigger and bare-@ never escapes markdown).
2. INPUT: The implemented, tested, gate-passing feature (P1.M2.T1.S1 config + P1.M2.T2.S1 markdown bare-@ + P1.M3.T1.S1 green gate). The existing README.md content.
3. LOGIC: Add to README.md: (a) A short subsection under the existing 'Syntax' heading (after the Markdown-imports bullet list) titled something like 'Optional: bare-`@` markdown imports' — state it is OFF by default, enabled by setting `{"markdownBareAtImports": true}` in `file-injector.json`; the two locations (global `~/.pi/agent/file-injector.json`, project `.pi/file-injector.json`); the project file is honored only in a trusted project; when on, a bare `@file.md` inside a delivered markdown file is treated exactly like `#@file.md` (same relative-only / code-exempt / dedup / shorthand rules), and `#@` still works and is never double-matched. (b) A one-line note in 'Limits' that bare-@ is markdown-only (the prompt is unaffected). (c) Keep it consistent with the existing 'no config by default' framing (§13.4): emphasize this is the ONE opt-in. Do NOT duplicate the full §4.6 spec — README is an overview, not a reference. Match the existing voice and code-fence style.
4. OUTPUT: A README.md that surfaces the new capability coherently alongside the existing feature docs. No stale claims.
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This subtask IS the changeset-level documentation sync (the final sweep). Per-symbol JSDoc already rode with M1/M2 (Mode A); this handles the whole-feature overview.
